content.full_text:
July 13, 1992

John Wallace, Esquire  
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
428 "J" Street  
Suite 800  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Mr. Wallace:

I represent the Capistrano Bay Park and Recreation District, a public agency. The District has a five person elected Board of Directors and owns a number of parks in Dana Point, California.

The Board is now considering purchasing a building which was one of the first buildings constructed in Dana Point, in the 1920's. The building would be purchased for $1,100,000. It would then be leased to the Dana Point Historical Society and other non-profit users, such as the Chamber of Commerce.

It has been brought to my attention that one or more of the Directors may be a dues-paying member of either the Historical Society or the Chamber of Commerce, or both.

The question is whether their payment of annual dues to these non-profit organizations precludes them from voting on the acquisition and the subsequent leasing of the building, presumably to the non-profits mentioned.

I would appreciate a written response, as I am quite sure the issue will be raised.

Thank you for your assistance and if there are any questions, please call.

Yours truly,

William M. Wilcoxen
